title: correct_form.md
findings: Document is 56% DOG-LATIN — predominantly addresses corporate fictions / dead entities.
JURISDICTION MIXING detected — DOG-LATIN and English on the same document. Per Chicago Manual of Styles Art. 11:147, creates a jurisdictional void.
Contains past tense markers — past tense = dead time = fiction. Cannot contract with the dead.
Contains modal/future verbs (SHALL, WILL, WOULD, etc.) — future tense = fiction. Cannot contract with what hasn't happened.
recommendations: Replace all DOG-LATIN (unhyphenated all-caps) with either English proper nouns or parse-syntax correct :Colon-Hyphen: forms.
A document must use ONE consistent jurisdiction. Choose either parse-syntax (:Colon-Hyphen:) or English, never DOG-LATIN.
Convert all past tense to now-time gerund (-ING) form.
Remove all modal verbs. State facts in now-time: 'FOR THE CLAIMING...' not 'shall claim...'

title: court_order.md
findings: Document contains 24% DOG-LATIN — addresses corporate fiction alongside living text.
Found 10 null adverb-verb chain(s) — these constructions convey ZERO facts.
JURISDICTION MIXING detected — DOG-LATIN and English on the same document. Per Chicago Manual of Styles Art. 11:147, creates a jurisdictional void.
Contains past tense markers — past tense = dead time = fiction. Cannot contract with the dead.
Contains modal/future verbs (SHALL, WILL, WOULD, etc.) — future tense = fiction. Cannot contract with what hasn't happened.
Contains pronouns — pronouns REMOVE the fact. 'He shall pay' — WHO is 'he'? No fact established.
recommendations: Replace adverb-verb chains with noun-fact constructions. A sentence without nouns carries no facts.
A document must use ONE consistent jurisdiction. Choose either parse-syntax (:Colon-Hyphen:) or English, never DOG-LATIN.
Convert all past tense to now-time gerund (-ING) form.
Remove all modal verbs. State facts in now-time: 'FOR THE CLAIMING...' not 'shall claim...'
Replace all pronouns with actual nouns/names.
